task:
ISS-002: Implement LSP client for precise call-edge detection in gid-core code_graph module.

Context:
- Current code_graph.rs (7039 lines) uses tree-sitter for structure extraction + name-matching heuristics for call edges
- Name-matching produces ~28K call edges with many false positives (e.g., different types with same method name get linked)
- Need to add LSP client that starts language servers (typescript-language-server, rust-analyzer, pyright) via stdio
- LSP client sends textDocument/definition requests for each call site found by tree-sitter
- Results replace/augment the name-matched call edges with compiler-precise edges (confidence 1.0)

Architecture decided:
- New file: crates/gid-core/src/lsp_client.rs — lightweight LSP client (initialize, definition queries, shutdown)
- Modify code_graph.rs: after tree-sitter pass, optionally run LSP pass to refine call edges
- CLI flag: `gid extract --lsp` to enable LSP-enhanced extraction
- tree-sitter provides structure (files, classes, functions, imports), LSP provides precise call edges + type info
- Start with TypeScript support (typescript-language-server --stdio), then Rust (rust-analyzer) and Python (pyright)

Target codebase for testing: /Users/potato/clawd/projects/claude-code-source-code (1902 files, 512K lines TypeScript)
Project source: /Users/potato/clawd/projects/gid-rs/
Main crate: crates/gid-core/

Key files:
- crates/gid-core/src/code_graph.rs — current extraction logic
- crates/gid-core/src/lib.rs — module declarations
- ISSUES.md — ISS-002 description
- docs/DESIGN.md, docs/PRODUCT-ROADMAP.md — architecture docs
